title: quercus undulata var. breviloba
intro: Quercus undulata var. breviloba, also known as the rough-leafed evergreen oak, is a plant species that belongs to the family Fagaceae and is native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, providing health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. The leaves of Quercus undulata var. breviloba can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and have also been used in traditional medicine as a poultice for skin conditions and as a topical treatment for wounds.
